queries: persona YAML fixture test model metadata

build_from.reason: Defines RegistryBaseURL (line 24, currently hardcoded to https://raw.githubusercontent.com/atcr/personas/main) and BaseURL(), the ATCR_PERSONAS_URL override resolver — the exact symbol AC1's URL repoint (atcr/personas -> samestrin/atcr + in-repo community path) must change. It also owns FetchIndex/FetchPersonaYAML, the functions the fetch-and-pin mechanism (AC1) and mock-registry end-to-end tests (AC6) extend.
build_from.suggested_approach: Repoint RegistryBaseURL to samestrin/atcr (e.g. https://raw.githubusercontent.com/samestrin/atcr/main/personas/community). Keep the existing env-override precedence in BaseURL(). Add Provider/Model/Tasks/Tags fields to PersonaIndexEntry in internal/personas/search.go and extend Search() with structural matching plus optional model/provider filters. Wire --model/--provider flags onto cmd/atcr/personas.go:newPersonasSearchCmd. For AC1's fetch-and-pin, change cmd/atcr/init.go and cmd/atcr/quickstart.go so they install community personas via internal/personas.Install (using the pinned version present in the fetched YAML's version field) instead of copying embedded built-in .md files; preserve backward compatibility for users with existing .atcr/personas/ files.

existing_patterns.name: Injectable HTTPClient + httptest for network code
existing_patterns.description: internal/personas/client.go defines a minimal HTTPClient interface (Do(req) (*http.Response, error)) satisfied by *http.Client; cmd/atcr/personas.go holds a package-level personasClient var that tests can swap. All fetch tests run against httptest.NewServer with ATCR_PERSONAS_URL pointed at it — zero live network calls in CI.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Any new fetch-and-pin / offline-behavior code (AC1) and the mock-registry end-to-end test (AC6) — reuse this exact injection pattern rather than inventing a new HTTP seam.

existing_patterns.name: Env-var override with hardcoded default
existing_patterns.description: BaseURL() in client.go: strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(envPersonasURL)) wins when non-empty, else RegistryBaseURL constant. Simple, no config file involved.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Keep this exact override precedence when repointing the default (AC1) — do not add a config.yaml layer for this.

existing_patterns.name: Persona YAML as registry-agent superset
existing_patterns.description: docs/personas-authoring.md defines the authoring contract in full: provider+model are REQUIRED (validated strictly against the registry agent schema), name/version/description are OPTIONAL catalog-only keys ignored by the registry loader, and an optional language field drives skeptic routing. A persona also requires a prompt template (Markdown, in personas/) with mandatory ## Role/## Output Format sections and a fixed set of template variables, plus a .patch fixture in personas/testdata/ named <slug>_fixture.patch.
existing_patterns.follow_for: AC3 (author the model-indexed library) and AC7/AC8 (fixture-enforced authoring contract) — every new persona YAML + prompt + fixture must follow this exact three-part structure, and the new provider/model-in-structured-metadata rule (AC2) must be added to this same document as a checklist item, not a parallel doc.

existing_patterns.name: Round-robin model binding in quickstart
existing_patterns.description: internal/quickstart/manifest.go RegistryYAML() binds model := m.Models[i%len(m.Models)] per roster persona index — no lens-to-model matching, just cycling through the manifest's model list. This is the 'generic, not tuned' binding the epic's Problem Statement #3 calls out.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Reference only for this epic — the epic's Out of Scope explicitly excludes a registry.yaml task-to-model mapping schema, so this round-robin logic itself is not being replaced; the model-indexed persona library (AC3) is a separate, install-time mechanism, not a quickstart rewire.

existing_patterns.name: Six-subcommand persona CLI wired through internal/personas
existing_patterns.description: cmd/atcr/personas.go's newPersonasCmd registers install/list/search/remove/test/upgrade, each a thin cobra RunE that calls the matching internal/personas function (Install, List, Search, Remove, TestPersona, Upgrade) using package-level personasClient/personasDir/personasFixtureRunner vars for test injection.
existing_patterns.follow_for: AC2's --model/--provider search filters: add flags to newPersonasSearchCmd() the same way --scores is added to newPersonasListCmd(), and extend internal/personas.Search's signature/logic rather than adding a parallel search function.

existing_patterns.name: Bundle expansion from embedded manifests
existing_patterns.description: internal/personas/bundles.go parses embedded bundle manifests under internal/personas/bundles/*.yaml and installs each member persona via Install. It skips already-present members and continues past individual failures.
existing_patterns.follow_for: If the model-indexed library ships a convenience bundle (e.g. bundle/community-library), add a new manifest here and wire it through cmd/atcr/personas.go's installBundle path.

existing_patterns.name: Built-in persona registry embedded via //go:embed
existing_patterns.description: personas/personas.go lists canonical names, serves embedded .md templates, and provides Fixtures for the subset that have personas/testdata/<name>_fixture.patch. It panics at init if the embedded files do not match the names slice.
existing_patterns.follow_for: AC4's human-name migration (sentinel->sasha, tracer->penny, idiomatic->ingrid) must update the names slice and the embedded file set; the stragglers' prompts move to the in-repo community layout, so decide whether they remain built-in or become community-only.

related_files.purpose: Owns RegistryBaseURL, BaseURL(), FetchIndex, FetchPersonaYAML — the fetch/URL layer AC1 repoints and AC1's fetch-and-pin logic extends.

Defines PersonaIndexEntry{Name,Version,Description,Path} and Search() (name/description substring match only) — exactly what AC2 must extend with Provider/Model/Tasks/Tags fields and structured-metadata matching.

CLI surface for all six persona subcommands; renderPersonaSearch renders index.json search hits as a 3-column table (Name/Version/Description) that will need Provider/Model columns once AC2 lands, and newPersonasSearchCmd needs --model/--provider flags.

The complete authoring contract (YAML schema, prompt template, fixture, contribution checklist) every AC3 persona must satisfy, and the document AC8's all-human-names convention note must be added to.

End-user install guide documenting the six subcommands including the current search <keyword> name/description-only behavior; needs updating for --model/--provider filters (AC2) and the discover-by-model flow (Proposed Solution #4).

Currently copies embedded built-in personas into .atcr/personas/. AC1's fetch-and-pin requires it to install community personas from the repointed RegistryBaseURL instead (or in addition), writing validated YAML with a pinned version.

Currently reuses runInit to lay down .atcr/ and then writes a synthetic-bound registry.yaml for builtins.Names(). AC1 requires it to fetch-and-pin community personas and define the --offline stub behavior.

TemplateFixtureRunner only resolves fixtures for built-in personas today (community personas return HasFixture: false). AC7's fixture enforcement for the new library personas requires extending runner resolution to community/in-repo personas or ensuring the library ships as built-ins with embedded fixtures.

Fetches and validates a single persona YAML before atomic write. The fetch-and-pin path in init/quickstart will call this; no signature change expected, but ensure it handles the new community index path and offline errors gracefully.

Compares installed persona version to remote version. AC1's upgrade path already works via the version field; the repointed URL and new index schema propagate automatically once client.go/search.go change.

PersonaMeta carries Version for community personas, which is the pin value displayed by list and compared by upgrade. The discovery note about using PersonaMeta for pinning is accurate: the installed YAML's version field is the pin, and list.go reads it.

Round-robin persona-to-model binding for atcr quickstart's generated registry.yaml — background context for the Problem Statement's 'generic, not tuned' gap; not directly rewired by this epic (see Out of Scope).

Shared prompt-template scaffold ({{.AgentName}}, {{.ScopeRule}}) that built-in and library personas' prompts follow; relevant to authoring the new model-indexed personas (AC3) and the straggler renames (AC4).

Canonical built-in persona registry. AC4's migration of sentinel/tracer/idiomatic to sasha/penny/ingrid must update the names slice and embedded file expectations, or remove the stragglers if they become community-only.

Directory holding built-in persona prompt Markdown (personas/bruce.md, personas/sentinel.md, etc.) and personas/testdata/ fixtures; the straggler renames (sentinel->sasha, tracer->penny, idiomatic->ingrid) and new in-repo community-persona content land here or under personas/community/.

Quickstart section (referenced at README.md:59) documents atcr quickstart as the first-run path; AC5 requires reordering/annotating this section to lead with Synthetic and reference DashScope/Chutes/Featherless/LiteLLM per the onboarding hierarchy.

integration_points.description: Single choke point for the default fetch URL; AC1's repoint changes only the RegistryBaseURL constant here, and every caller (install/search/list/upgrade/init/quickstart) picks it up automatically.

Schema + matcher AC2 extends: add Provider/Model/Tasks/Tags fields to the struct (json tags matching the future index.json generation) and add structural matching to Search alongside the existing substring match.
Where --model/--provider flags get added and where the new Provider/Model columns are rendered, following the same cmd.Flags().Bool pattern already used on newPersonasListCmd.

AC7/AC8's enforceable convention (bound model in structured metadata; human-names-only) is documented as new checklist items here, mirroring how existing checklist items map 1:1 to fixture-test assertions.

AC1's fetch-and-pin behavior: init/quickstart must install community personas via internal/personas.Install (using the fetched YAML's version field as the pin) instead of copying embedded built-ins, while preserving backward compatibility for existing .atcr/personas/ workspaces.

reusable_components.name: HTTPClient injection + httptest pattern
reusable_components.usage_example: Any new fetch-and-pin logic (AC1) or the mock-registry end-to-end test (AC6) constructs an httptest.NewServer, sets ATCR_PERSONAS_URL, and calls the existing FetchIndex/FetchPersonaYAML/Search functions unchanged.

reusable_components.name: TemplateFixtureRunner
reusable_components.usage_example: Currently only built-in personas have fixtures. AC7 requires either extending the runner to resolve in-repo/community fixtures or shipping the library personas as built-ins with embedded personas/testdata/<slug>_fixture.patch files.

architecture_notes: The community-persona mechanism (Epic 9.0) is already fully generic over the repo it fetches from — repointing AC1 is a one-constant change (RegistryBaseURL) plus an in-repo community-persona directory layout to serve from, not a new fetch client.

PersonaIndexEntry today ignores unknown JSON fields on decode (encoding/json default behavior), so adding Provider/Model/Tasks/Tags to the struct is additive and backward-compatible with any index.json that predates the schema change — satisfies AC1's backward-compatibility requirement for existing on-disk personas.

The persona YAML schema is already validated as 'a superset of a registry agent' with provider/model REQUIRED — the AC2/AC7 structured-metadata requirement is really about propagating fields the schema already mandates into index.json's generation step, not inventing new validation.

internal/quickstart/manifest.go's round-robin binding is architecturally separate from the community-persona install path (internal/personas/*) — the epic's Out of Scope confirms quickstart's binding algorithm is not touched; AC3's model-indexed library is discovered/installed via atcr personas search/install, independent of quickstart's roster generation.

init and quickstart currently embed built-in personas as .md files under .atcr/personas/. AC1's fetch-and-pin changes this to install community YAML personas (via internal/personas.Install) so the canonical source is the fetched repo, not the binary. Backward compatibility must handle existing .atcr/personas/ directories that contain .md files rather than YAML.

The straggler rename (sentinel->sasha, tracer->penny, idiomatic->ingrid) and the new model-indexed personas must not create a mixed naming state. Decide whether the renamed stragglers stay built-in (updating personas/personas.go) or move entirely to the in-repo community layout.

files_to_modify.reason: Repoint RegistryBaseURL to samestrin/atcr + in-repo community path (AC1).
Add Provider/Model/Tasks/Tags to PersonaIndexEntry and extend Search with structural matching / model/provider filters (AC2).
Add --model/--provider flags to personas search; update renderPersonaSearch to surface the new columns (AC2).
Replace embedded built-in persona copy with fetch-and-pin of community personas from the repointed RegistryBaseURL, preserving backward compatibility for existing .atcr/personas/ workspaces (AC1).
Fetch-and-pin community personas instead of wiring built-ins into the synthetic registry, and define --offline stub behavior (AC1).
Extend TemplateFixtureRunner so the new library personas (whether built-in or in-repo community) can have passing fixtures, enabling AC7's fixture-enforced model-in-metadata rule.

Update the canonical names slice and embedded file expectations for the straggler rename (sentinel->sasha, tracer->penny, idiomatic->ingrid) per AC4, or remove stragglers if they become community-only.
Document the provider/model-in-structured-metadata authoring rule (AC2/AC7) and the all-human-names convention (AC4/AC8).
Document --model/--provider search filters and the discover-by-model flow (AC2, Proposed Solution #4).
Reorder/annotate Quickstart to lead with atcr quickstart (Synthetic), document DashScope/Chutes/Featherless/LiteLLM per the onboarding hierarchy, position majors personas as opt-in (AC5).

files_to_create.purpose: The in-repo canonical index the repointed RegistryBaseURL serves, carrying Provider/Model-structured entries for every library persona (AC1, AC2, AC3).
files_to_create.based_on: existing PersonaIndexEntry schema in internal/personas/search.go, extended per AC2

files_to_create.purpose: New human-named, model-tuned persona YAML files for the library (AC3) covering frontier providers (Claude/GPT/Gemini) and flat-rate open models (DeepSeek/Qwen/Kimi/GLM). Each carries provider/model plus version/description/tasks/tags metadata.
Prompt template per new community persona, phrased per the target model's vendor prompting guide (AC3).

Fixture per new library persona proving its target category is caught (AC3/AC7). If personas ship as community-only, the fixture runner must be extended to load these; if they remain built-ins, embed them here.
New tests for Provider/Model/Tasks/Tags filtering, case-insensitive model/provider matching, and backward-compatibility with old-shape index.json (AC2, AC6).

test_patterns.example_test: internal/personas/personas_test.go (httptest-based fetch tests; add new cases for model/provider search and backward-compatible old-shape index.json parsing)

integration_gaps.description: The plan says 'samestrin/atcr (+ in-repo path)' but does not fix the directory name. Pick a path (e.g. personas/community/) and update RegistryBaseURL, FetchPersonaYAML, FetchIndex, and the created index.json/YMAL/Markdown files consistently.

AC1 requires init/quickstart to obtain personas by fetch-and-pin, but today they copy embedded .md templates into .atcr/personas/. Decide whether to (a) install community YAML there instead, (b) keep .md editable copies alongside fetched YAML, or (c) write fetched YAML to ~/.config/atcr/personas/ while keeping .atcr/personas/ for project overrides. The backward-compatibility story for existing workspaces must be explicit.

integration_gaps.topic: Fixture runner coverage for community/in-repo personas
integration_gaps.description: TemplateFixtureRunner currently returns HasFixture: false for any non-built-in name. AC7's fixture test must enforce the model-in-metadata rule for the new library personas, so the runner must either resolve fixtures for in-repo/community personas or the library must remain built-in with embedded fixtures.

AC4 migrates sentinel/tracer/idiomatic to sasha/penny/ingrid. Decide whether the renamed personas stay in the built-in set (updating personas/personas.go and embedded files) or move to personas/community/ (removing them from the binary). This affects init/quickstart, list, remove, and test behavior.

AC1 mentions a --offline stub. Decide whether this is a new global flag, a personas subcommand flag, or simply graceful error messaging when fetch fails because the repo is not yet public or the user has no network.

No tooling currently generates personas/community/index.json from the YAML files. Decide whether to hand-maintain it, add a generation script/cmd, or validate it in CI. This is important because drift between YAML provider/model and index.json provider/model would break AC2/AC7.
